free_text: Cross sections of proton induced reactions on natSb
High purity thin Sb targets of natural isotopic
composition (Goodfellow, 99.5%) were prepared by vacuum
evaporation onto 25 micron thick kapton (polyimide,
Goodfellow) backing.
High purity thin
Sb targets with natural isotopic composition were
vacuum evaporated onto kapton backing. 19 sandwich
targets (kapton-Sb-Sb-kapton) were made with an
average thickness of 0.6 mg/cm2. The kapton foil
acted also as catcher foil of the recoiled reaction
products. The beam energy was 36 MeV, the incident
proton beam was about 135 nA, irradiation time was
1.0 h. The beam was monitored by and Ti monitor
foils.
Chapter 4
The initial beam uncertainty was +-0.3 MeV.
The uncertainty of the beam energy throughout
the stack increases to 1.6 MeV at the last foil.
The overall uncertainty amounted to 12-15%.
surface density of target nuclei (5%)
flux of incident charged particles (9%)
nuclear decay and branching data (4%)
detector efficiency  (4-9%)
statistical and peak fitting (1-5%),
The uncertainty of the cross-sections were
estimated taking the independent relative errors of the
linearly contributing components.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
